blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Where is the swordsman's center of gravity? The answer is outside. In schema terms, this is called 'external gravity.' Repeat after me—external gravity.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: External gravity!
The cadets shouted in unison, but their voices were laced with anger.
A term they had never heard in an entire semester was being explained right before the exam.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: The reason I never explained it before is because I couldn't be bothered. Of course, idiots like you wouldn't understand anyway. But today, since I'm in a bad mood, I've decided to add an evaluation on external gravity. Understood?

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Yes!
The cadets' response echoed loudly.
Their voices filled with suppressed fury.
Changing the evaluation criteria now meant all their hard work over the past six months was for nothing.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: Ah, I really want to kill him. What kind of teacher is that?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I'll demonstrate only once. Pay close attention and apply it to your evaluation.
Whatever their thoughts, the Colosseum, already quiet, fell into complete silence.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Cut while advancing, cut while retreating.
Kuan demonstrated slashes while alternating between forward and backward movements.
His limp made it look unimpressive.
But the sound of the blade cutting through the air was terrifying.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: This is the basic movement of swordsmanship. Physical strength shifts the center of gravity, and the shift in center enhances the blade's destructive power. Moreover, if you amplify it with schema, the blade's power can increase exponentially.
The cadets gulped.
Though they had heard this countless times, their tension was at its peak with the exam looming.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: ……That's what low-level thinkers like you would assume. But if you approach real combat with that mindset, you'll die within three seconds.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: What an asshole.
The cadets' glares intensified.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Remember this. No matter how strong your destructive power is, if your movement is inferior, the outcome is already decided. Therefore, swordsmen adjust their center of gravity based on the situation. For example, like this.
Kuan stomped the ground with his foot.
Then, as he lifted one leg, his body tilted toward the ground at an angle of over 70 degrees.
The cadets stared in disbelief.
Without ropes tied around his waist, maintaining such a posture should have been impossible for a human.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: The amplified force from schema maximizes the shift in the center of gravity. Right now, my center of gravity is outside my body. This is called external gravity.
Kuan's body snapped upright like a roly-poly toy.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: With external gravity, the possibilities of movement become limitless. For example, this is also possible.
Kuan stomped the ground again and leaned backward until his back nearly touched the ground.
His body rotated in a conical motion.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: External gravity isn't actual gravity. It's just an extreme form of inertia. So, if you apply rotation to external gravity, your body will rotate too.
As Kuan extended his sword outward, the rotation slowed.
But as the tilt corrected itself, centrifugal force accelerated the spin.
Whoosh—!
Kuan, now standing vertically, spun at an incredible speed before stopping abruptly in a poised stance.
The cadets stared in awe.
If they had been in his place, their ankles would have been sliced off first, followed by their bodies being shredded into dozens of pieces by the accelerating blade.
Defense was impossible.
Just as humans couldn't stop a tornado caused by atmospheric pressure differences, this explosive external gravity was akin to a rotating blade machine.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: This is the Death Magician.
A nickname given because his opponents died without even understanding how.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: This is today's practical evaluation. The first part is obstacle clearance, and the second is urban combat movement.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: Instructor, I have a question.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Drop. Two hundred push-ups, now.
The cadet's face paled as he dropped and began the push-ups.
He wondered what he had done wrong.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Remember this. To get the answer you want, you must overwhelm the other person. I'm not your parent. Don't stare at me with those desperate eyes begging for praise. It's disgusting. Understood?

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Yes! We'll correct ourselves!

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Try again.
After finishing the punishment, the cadet raised his hand with a grimace.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: Instructor! I have a question!

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Speak.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: What does the urban combat movement evaluate?

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: That's what I'm about to explain.
The cadet felt foolish.
His motivation for asking was simply to impress Kuan, but Kuan saw even respect as a sign of weakness.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Urban combat movement. I'll keep it short since I can't be bothered.
Kuan entered a building with an open front, like a dollhouse, making it easy for everyone to observe.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: External gravity shines in complex terrain. Urban combat, in particular, is confined to tight spaces, so the level of movement decides the outcome.
Kuan stomped the floor but didn't use external gravity.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Stomping the ground generates force to shift your center. This is called the 'first impact.' Of course, in real combat, you won't have time for such theatrics, but for beginners like you, stomping is advisable.
Though laced with mockery, the cadets were too awed by Kuan's skill to feel angry anymore.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: But I don't use the first impact by stomping. If I did, the floor would shatter. Schema offers various first impact methods for external gravity, but I'll use the most basic one—muscle vibration.
Kuan contracted his upper body muscles to generate vibrations, then leaped and touched the wall.
By bending his elbows to adjust inertia, he hovered in mid-air for over three seconds.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Whoa!
The cadets gasped in amazement.
Normally, Kuan would have ordered them to drop for push-ups, but he ignored it and continued.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Create external gravity with the first impact and leap. Of course, in this case, external gravity would act toward the wall. Applying this, movements like this are possible.
Kuan walked along the wall.
The cadets slowly raised their heads, staring in disbelief as he stood upside down on the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: External gravity ultimately uses inertial force to cancel gravity. So, right now, gravity isn't acting on my body.
Kuan walking on the ceiling was beyond wondrous.
It was bizarre.
But this wasn't magic.
Even circus performers could run along walls without schema.
Kuan simply maximized inertia with stronger and more precise force.
When the external gravity dissipated, Kuan fell in accordance with gravity.
By the time he finished the demonstration and stepped outside, the cadets' expressions had completely changed.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: All of you, drop. Two hundred push-ups, now.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Yes, sir!
The cadets dropped with dismay.
For regular students, most could handle schema, so two hundred push-ups wasn't impossible.
But for first-years like them, whose reinforcement capabilities were limited, it was a grueling punishment.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Listen while you work. The first practical is horizontal obstacles, and the height decreases as the stages progress. The final stage is impossible to clear without external gravity. Time limit: twenty seconds. The obstacles are made with real blades, so failure could mean death. Of course, I doubt any of you would be foolish enough to dodge them. If injured, Instructor Kaina will treat you.
Kaina was the best surgeon at the swordsmanship school.
If mages had healers, swordsmen had surgeons who specialized in battlefield medicine.
The problem was the excruciating pain of the procedures, especially for amputees, who often passed out from the agony of nerve reconnection.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: Damn it! Hearing that just makes it scarier!

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Urban combat movement is simple. Create external gravity and rely on the wall. The longer you last, the higher your score. Understood?

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Yes!
Kuan observed the cadets' sharpened expressions before collecting the evaluation sheets and moving on.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Number one, start now. I won't call names, so come out when it's your turn. The rest of you, rest.
Rian sat down, propping his chin on his hand.
With each person taking at least three minutes, there was plenty of time.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: How do you feel? Think you can do it?
A female voice—rare in the swordsmanship school.
But Rian turned his head as if it were nothing special.
The speaker was a beautiful woman with wavy golden hair, tall and slender with long limbs.
Elzien Tess.
The only daughter of the prestigious Elzien family, she had enrolled in the swordsmanship school to follow in the footsteps of her father, a colonial commander.
Her face could be seen as either cute or fierce, but her personality was undeniably fierce.
Her skills were exceptional, unmatched among the first-years, and she never backed down, even among the male cadets.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Sit down quickly. If the instructor catches you, it's execution.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Oh? Even the great Rian is scared of the instructor? He's too busy grading to notice.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What do you know? He has eyes in the back. Why did you move seats anyway?

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: What are friends for? I came to cheer you up in case you were nervous.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Since when have you seen me nervous?

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Heh. Well, with nowhere left to fall, I guess there's nothing to be nervous about. The bottom rank is quite comfortable, isn't it?
Most cadets would have been offended, but Rian just pursed his lips.
Tess smiled.
His temperament, unfazed by most things, set him apart from the others.
Why was such a guy stuck at the bottom?
Among the first-years, Rian trained the hardest.
In fact, his training regimen was almost brutal in its intensity.
Yet his grades never improved.
A month ago, rumors spread that he had finally grasped schema, but his reinforcement speed was so slow it was hard to believe.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Even if I'm at the bottom, you never know until you try. Who knows? Things might work out unexpectedly.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Heh, that's so like you.
Tess had never seen Rian nervous.
Even before a test where limbs could be severed, the weakest among them remained composed—a peculiar feeling.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Well, he was like that back then too.
Tess had met Rian during the probation period.
Swordsmen were naturally inclined toward combat.
With 300 such individuals gathered, the atmosphere turned hostile from day one.
The instructors didn't interfere with dormitory affairs, and soon, a few bullies emerged.
The hierarchy was established in an instant.
Those who had mastered schema formed the first group, those with strong physical abilities the second, and the rest the third.
The problem was those who didn't even make it into the third group—boys who had no interest in swordsmanship but were forced into the academy by their parents.
Richard Feiger, the leader of the first group, relentlessly tormented them.
Naturally violent and now in a school that legally taught killing techniques, he had no restraints left.
Tess didn't care.
Though technically part of the first group, the ten or so female cadets were exempt from the hierarchy.
At the time, Rian was in the middle of the second group.
Not a position to be bullied, but Rian kept to himself.
His reason for joining the swordsmanship school was singular: graduation didn't matter.
He just needed to grow stronger to become a swordsman worthy of Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Hahaha! Hey, run! Faster!

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Heehee! Heehee!
One day, the first group cadets mounted the weaker boys' backs and held a race.
They whipped the "horses" with sticks, eliciting horse-like cries with each strike.
Some frowned, but most cheered as if watching a real horse race.
Small bets were placed, and anyone who showed displeasure risked becoming the next target.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Faster! I'm winning! I'm winning!
Feiger, leading the race, was ecstatic.
True to his nature, he never tolerated defeat, even in games.
As the finish line approached, his strikes grew fiercer.
The "horse" crawled on bleeding knees, tears streaming.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Heehee! Heehee!

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Yes! I'm first……!
As Feiger was about to cross the finish line, a blur flashed before his eyes, followed by a sharp pain in his nose.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Ow!
Before he knew it, Feiger had been kicked off his "horse" and onto the ground.
The other racers surged past him.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Know your limits. This isn't your playground.
Feiger clutched his throbbing nose and glared at the blue-haired boy.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: You bastard……!
Ozente Rian.
Feiger knew him as a mediocre second-grouper.
The fact that he'd been struck by someone who hadn't even mastered schema wounded his pride.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: What's your problem? You wanna die?
Rian didn't respond.
His reticent nature hadn't changed since joining the school.
If he had nothing to say, he wouldn't force words.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: This guy…….
Feiger rose, radiating killing intent.
The situation was explosive, but the terrified cadets didn't intervene.
Of course, some saw no need to.
Tess was one of them.
This was a swordsmanship school. In a place where combat skills were taught, standing up for the weak changed nothing. The hierarchy would prevail.

blocks.speaker: Female Cadet
blocks.content: Oh? It's Rian. He finally caused trouble.
Tess glanced back.
Another female cadet, taller than most boys, stood like a wall.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: You know his name? He doesn't seem strong.

blocks.speaker: Female Cadet
blocks.content: He's weak. But stubborn. Last time, the second group pooled money for Feiger's birthday. Rian was the only one who refused.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Ah, so he's marked.

blocks.speaker: Female Cadet
blocks.content: Feiger's been waiting for this. But I'm surprised. I thought he was quiet, but he's got a temper.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Hmm……
Now Tess studied Rian closely.
He remained silent but showed no intention of backing down.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: There really are all kinds. The kingdom is vast.
Her interest ended there.
Having grown up around eccentrics due to her father's role as a colonial commander, she knew one truth in this world: without strength, even the noblest beliefs would break.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Heh. This guy's gone mute? Too late for regrets. Did you really think you could walk away unharmed after this?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Let's take this outside. If the instructors find out, it'll affect our evaluations.
Feiger was dumbfounded.
Instead of begging for mercy, Rian was suggesting they fight discreetly to avoid instructor attention.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Fine, have it your way. Follow me. The rest of you, come too. I'll show you what happens when you cross me.
Feiger led Rian to a clearing behind the building.
The first, second, and third groups stood behind Feiger, while only the outcasts stood with Rian.
But they had already lost their will to fight.
No, they had never intended to.
They just weren't part of Feiger's clique and secretly resented Rian for escalating things.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Hey, if you don't want a beating, switch sides now.
Feiger offered amnesty.
The cadets didn't hesitate.
They rushed past Rian to the other side.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Hahaha! Pathetic. Hey, Rian. What now? Everyone's abandoned you.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Relying on numbers? You were never fit to be a knight from the start.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: What's that?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If you have no choice but to fight, then fight. That's what a knight does. You don't even deserve the title.
Feiger snorted.
In war, knights tore at each other like dogs. The title was just a glorified label for those who survived.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Fine, let's see if your fists match your mouth. I'll take you on myself. Don't get comfortable. Fighting alone means I'll kill you.
Rian raised his fists into a fighting stance.
Feiger, meanwhile, sauntered forward casually.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: I'll let you hit me first. Go ahead.
As Feiger beckoned, Rian threw a punch.
Feiger dodged and struck Rian's abdomen.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Guh!
Rian's eyes widened as searing pain tore through his stomach.
It felt like a boulder had been driven into him.
Was this the power of schema-enhanced strikes?

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Idiot. Why would I let you hit me?
Though Feiger mocked Rian, he was surprised the first strike hadn't ended it.
He'd underestimated him.
With that in mind, he unleashed a flurry of blows.
Rian was in agony.
Though it was a fistfight, schema itself was a lethal weapon.
It felt like being pummeled by a heavily armored soldier.
Feiger's fist buried itself in Rian's stomach, lifting him off the ground.
When he landed, even Rian's endurance couldn't keep him standing.
His knees buckled, and he crashed to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: As expected…….
Feiger sneered down at Rian.
In that instant, Rian shot forward like an arrow, closing the distance.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: What the—? He's still standing?
Feiger was stunned.
Rian hadn't mastered schema.
The feedback from his punches confirmed they had landed.
Without schema for defense, Rian should have been unconscious by now.
Rian targeted Feiger's stomach.
Aiming for the face would have been easily dodged.
But by closing in and blocking Feiger's vision, he made counterattacks difficult.
The strategy worked.
Feiger staggered back under the assault, his abdomen throbbing with each hit.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: What's with this guy?
For Feiger, winning was the bare minimum.
Being forced into a brawl with someone who couldn't even use schema was humiliating.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: You bastard!
Feiger lost his temper.
He grabbed Rian's shoulders, shoved him back, and rained down punches.
Dull thuds echoed across the clearing.
The cadets watched in horror as the one-sided beating continued.
When Feiger regained his senses, Rian lay crumpled like a rag.
Yet he still hadn't passed out.
As Rian's fingers twitched, Feiger's eyes burned with rage.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Argh! So annoying!
Feiger kicked Rian repeatedly.
Finally realizing the severity, the first group rushed in.

blocks.speaker: First Group Cadets
blocks.content: Hey, stop! You'll kill him!

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Let go! I'll kill him! Die, you bastard!
Rian's movements slowed until he finally lay still.
Feiger scanned the crowd.
The terror on their faces soothed his anger slightly.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Listen up! This is what happens when you cross me! Bring me a rope!
Rian was stripped to his underwear.
His muscular body was covered in bruises.
Feiger, deciding to make an example of him, tied Rian up and hung him from a tall tree.
Rian dangled like a shrimp, blood dripping from his face.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Hahaha! Looks like a worm on a fishing hook! Serves you right for acting tough.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Heh. Ah, this is so embarrassing. Acting all high and mighty, now look at him.
Mockery rained from all sides.
It was as if they'd won a war.
Tess frowned.
No matter how weak the victim, subjecting someone to such humiliation was unacceptable.
And there were girls their age present.
What was Rian thinking now? How deeply had this scarred him?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ugh……
Rian regained consciousness.
He hadn't passed out but was far from okay.
The cadets giggled, treating him like a zoo exhibit.
Tess met Rian's gaze.
She tried to keep her expression neutral.
Pity would only hurt him more in this state.
But Rian surprised her by smiling and winking.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ah, my apologies. There are ladies present.
Tess maintained her composure, but something strange stirred inside her.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Hahaha! What's he saying now? Still putting on a brave face? He's just a fool.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Hey, hey! Be honest. After getting beaten like that, do you really think you look cool? You're a joke.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Embarrassed? Huh? Showing off in front of girls like that. I'd rather bite my tongue and die.
Rian looked down at the cadets.
Despite the taunts, his eyes were calm.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It's fine. Even if it's embarrassing.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Hahaha! Well, guys like you should live like that. Or are you a masochist? You're enjoying this, aren't you?
Rian spoke softly.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I'll forget it soon.
Silence fell.
An uneasy tension gripped the air.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I fought my hardest, so I'm not ashamed. With time, I might not even remember today. But…… will you all be able to forget?
No one answered.
Their hearts pounded.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: The sight of you all, tails between your legs without even fighting, will haunt you forever. And you'll regret it. 'If only I'd fought then. If only I'd stood up.' But you know, don't you? Time doesn't rewind.
The cadets' faces trembled.
This fear was different from Feiger's violence.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Defeat is fine. The shame of standing by your beliefs fades quickly. But the memory of cowering in fear will follow you forever. While eating, sleeping, even on the battlefield—you'll suffer. That's why a swordsman's conviction is life itself. Once broken, it never returns.
No one mocked Rian now.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No one wants to remember their cowardice. Especially not swordsmen. The day will come when recalling today will be worse than death. So end it here. If you want to stand tall in the future, then right now, in this moment……
Rian spat blood and roared.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Fight, you idiots!
Rian's cry pierced the cadets' hearts.
Their blood boiled.
Their skills varied, but their desire to be known as battlefield ghosts, supreme swordsmen, and knights of steel was the same.
The collective fighting spirit was palpable.
The air grew hot.
The fear in their eyes when looking at Feiger was gone.
Feiger and the first group sensed the danger and radiated killing intent.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: Shut up! No one cares what a loser says! Want to take me down? Then come at me! I'll make you just like him!
Tess smirked and toyed with her lips.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Hmm.
Ozente Rian.
Swordsmanship School Cadet.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Alright.
Tess leaped into action, soaring over Feiger.
She untied the rope, and Rian fell with a thud, curling up in pain before sitting cross-legged.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: You're Rian, right? I'm Tess. Let's fight together.
She offered a handshake, but Rian just stared.
Still bound, he couldn't move.
Tess's appearance sparked a reaction.
Though part of the female group, her skills were considered on par with Feiger's.

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: I'll fight too! I can't take this anymore!

blocks.speaker: Cadet
blocks.content: If even a girl's stepping up, we can't just stand here!
One by one, they rallied to Rian's side.
Soon, all but the first group stood together, shouting.

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Let's show them! Drive those brutes out of the school!

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Drive them out! Drive them out!
Feiger's group was surrounded by nearly 300 cadets.
Even with schema-enhanced strength, they couldn't fight such numbers.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: You bastards! You really wanna die?

blocks.speaker: Cadets
blocks.content: Then kill us if you can, you bastards!
A strong boy from the second group struck first.
Feiger's head snapped back, and as he scowled, dozens rushed him, slamming him to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: W-wait! Stop!
Feiger shouted, realizing the situation.
But the cadets' fury wasn't so easily quelled.
All around, first-group cadets met the same fate.
Even the outcasts joined in, beating them mercilessly.

blocks.speaker: Feiger
blocks.content: H-help! Instructor! Instructor!
Tess watched the chaos indifferently, then turned to Rian with a smile.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Heh, sorry? Feels like I just stuck a fork in a meal you prepared.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It's fine. Playing hero isn't my thing. That's why I called you over.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Oh? You really didn't plan this?
Rian looked puzzled, and Tess realized he was serious.
Her expression turned incredulous.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Are you an idiot? The instructors would never leave us unsupervised. They're watching everything right now.
Tess's guess was spot-on.
Feiger was expelled for fractures, and his accomplices were denied admission despite completing probation.
As for Rian…… despite his poor training performance, he was admitted to Kaizen Swordsmanship School as one of the top 30.
Recalling their first meeting, Tess grinned.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: That Rian is now~ stuck at the bottom.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shut up. Why bring up the past? Did I ask to be first?

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Heh, but it's impressive. Most would've been crushed by self-doubt, yet you train without missing a day.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Self-doubt? That's long gone. I graduated from that.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Still, aren't you worried? You need to graduate and make a name for yourself as a swordsman. At this rate, you'll get nowhere.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: ……You're really trying to break me, huh?

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: What? I'm genuinely worried. I'm curious—what drives you?
Rian looked up at the sky.
Compared to the others, his motivation was simpler.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It'd be nice to make a name, but it's not necessary. I just need to get stronger. For my lord.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Your lord? What are you talking about?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ah, I've sworn a knight's oath.
Tess's eyes widened.
It felt like asking a kid who he'd marry, only to hear he was already engaged.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Seriously? You're not even twenty?
A knight's oath was a lifelong pledge a swordsman could make only once.
Choosing a lord required careful consideration of their future and potential.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Are you sure? Who is it? Someone I know?
Tess assumed it must be someone famous—a war hero, a royal official, or at least a regional lord.
Surely, one wouldn't pledge their life to just anyone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Shirone? Hmm… I don't know that name. What does he do?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: My friend. He's at a magic school in our hometown.
Tess was dumbfounded.
A friend? And not even a full-fledged mage—a student.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: What were you thinking? No matter how close, a knight's oath is too much. Wait, unless……
A sudden thought made her uneasy.
There was one reason someone would swear an oath to an obscure, unpromising individual.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: This Shirone… is he a girl?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Are you insane? What's wrong with you? Even if I'm untalented, I wouldn't swear an oath for that.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Really? Hmm.
Tess sighed in relief.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Anyway, keep it quiet. I believe Shirone will become the greatest. That's why I've staked my life on it.
Today, Rian seemed different.
Bonds between boys were one thing, but a knight's oath was another level.
Shirone must have been extraordinary.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Who is he? Shirone…
Then, inspiration struck.
She'd been wondering how to broach the topic, and Shirone provided the perfect excuse.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Can I meet him? This Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What? Why do you want to meet my friend?

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: You talk him up so much, I'm curious. It's like securing a job before finishing school, right?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Even so, it's out of nowhere. Besides, Shirone's in Creas. It's far from here.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: So what? The semester's ending soon anyway. My family's in the colonies, so I've got no one to meet. Let's visit Creas. Oh, there's an island south of it, right? A tourist spot. I've always wanted to go. Let's go with Shirone.
Tess's words flowed smoothly, as if rehearsed.
Rian's attitude shifted.
He had no interest in traveling alone with Tess, but with Shirone, it was different.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hmm, with Shirone… But wouldn't it be awkward with three? I'm not exactly sociable. Especially with girls.
Encouraged by Rian's openness, Tess's eyes sparkled.
The usually indifferent Rian was changing before her eyes, stirring a hint of jealousy.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Tell Shirone to bring a friend. He must know girls at the magic school. A double date. How about it?
A double date.
It sounded fun.
With the semester ending, Rian had been thinking of ways to spend time with Shirone anyway.
There was no reason to refuse.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Well, I'll think about it.
His tone suggested he'd already agreed.
Tess nodded, satisfied.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Great. Then let's finish this final evaluation with a bang!
Most cadets had finished, and soon it was Tess's turn.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Number 47, Elzien Tess. Ready for evaluation!
At the starting line for the obstacle course, Tess shouted, and Kuan signaled with a curled finger.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Begin.
Tess sprinted through the obstacles, but her body froze at the final one—a blade obstacle lower than her seated height.
Though she'd mastered schema earlier than most, external gravity was new to her today.
Due to schema's effects on the body, mastering it during growth years was rare—and discouraged.
Tess calmly executed the first impact.
Stomping the ground, the shockwave traveled through her schema.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Shift the center of gravity outward!
External gravity wasn't magic—just overcoming gravity with powerful inertia.
Confident, Tess leaned back.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Got it!
Her body arced like a fan, slipping under the blade.
It wasn't luck.
All her training had led to this.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Number 47, Elzien Tess. Obstacle course complete!
As Tess raised her arms, the cadets applauded.
But her pounding heart drowned out the noise.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: I did it. It really works.

blocks.speaker: Elzien Tess
blocks.content: Even a temperamental wreck is still a teacher.
After acing the urban combat movement, Tess secured first place.
With few participants left, her score seemed unbeatable.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Number 52, Ozente Rian. Ready for evaluation!
At Rian's turn, Kuan frowned.
He expected nothing, yet this cadet always managed to intrigue him.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Go.
Rian charged forward, clearing non-schema obstacles flawlessly.
But the final one posed a problem.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: First impact!
He stomped and leaned back.
Simultaneously, Kuan's eyes sharpened.
Something felt off. The blade seemed to be aiming for his neck.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Huh?
Kuan lunged, kicking Rian in the face.
Rian rolled across the ground, struggling to rise.
As he finally sat up, the furious instructor loomed over him.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: You idiot! Are you insane? Want to die that badly? Where's your schema? You just walked into a blade!
Rian snapped upright, shouting.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: My apologies! But I did it! Schema…!

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: What nonsense? Do you even know what schema is? No schema means disqualification!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No! I succeeded! I passed the strength enhancement test!

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: What? Strength enhancement? Where's the lie…?
Strength enhancement was verified by lifting twice one's weight.
Scanning the area, Kuan found a suitable test—a large leftover stone from construction.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: You. Lift this.
Rian took a deep breath and approached.
Having barely passed the strength test before, he was nervous.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Huuuuh!
He hugged the stone and strained.
With trembling legs, he lifted it overhead.
Kuan pressed a finger to Rian's body.
The vibration of muscle fibers confirmed his suspicion, darkening his expression.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Damn it.
No schema.
In all his years teaching, he'd never seen such a talentless student.
Even the least gifted usually mastered schema with top-tier training.
But Rian hadn't.
Normally, such students were expelled.
The world had no shortage of aspiring swordsmen.
But Kuan couldn't bring himself to discard Rian.
There was something about him.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Just how much has he trained…?
He couldn't hate this kid.
No, as a swordsman, he respected such determination.
But one problem barred all progress—no talent. Not even a shred.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Rian, sorry to say, but you haven't mastered schema.
The cadets stared in disbelief.
This was the first time Kuan had ever apologized.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That can't be! I'm lifting this stone! What is this, then?
Kuan sighed.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: That's… just your ridiculously strong muscles.
Rian looked stunned.
The stone dropped with a thud.
Ozente Rian. Dead last confirmed.
